methodsAttempted.query: site:helsinki.fi University of Helsinki generative AI artificial intelligence guidance students assessment teaching
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found the official student and teaching instructions pages, official news, course listings, PDFs, and library material.
Firecrawl search confirmed the official student instruction page and related teaching and ground-rules pages.

methodsAttempted.query: site:helsinki.fi tekoäly opetus opinnot tekoälyn käyttö opiskelussa Helsingin yliopisto
methodsAttempted.notes: Finnish-language search located Finnish equivalents of the same official student and teaching instructions plus the cheating/plagiarism guidance.
Firecrawl search found IT Helpdesk instructions for University-supported generative AI services and security boundaries.
Firecrawl search found the University news announcement and the official research-integrity GenAI guidance page.

methodsAttempted.query: site:helsinki.fi "guidelines for the use of generative AI in research" Helsinki pdf OR article
methodsAttempted.notes: Search confirmed that the research-integrity guidance page is the better policy source than the related news announcement.

artifacts.summary: Official-source-only discovery found public University of Helsinki student, teaching, IT Helpdesk, and research-integrity guidance that directly supports staged claims. The Flamma general principles page redirected to University SSO and was not accessed. A Faculty of Medicine thesis PDF was official and AI-specific but rejected for university-wide claims because it elaborates on faculty-level thesis guidance.

artifacts.claimText: University of Helsinki guidance says large language models may generally be used in teaching and writing support, while course teachers can restrict or prohibit use on pedagogical grounds and AI cannot be used in maturity tests.

artifacts.claimText: University of Helsinki research guidance supports responsible and critical use of GenAI in research, but says researchers are responsible for outputs, should verify them, describe substantial GenAI use, and must not list GenAI as a co-author.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: The University of Helsinki has two general-purpose generative AI solutions: Microsoft Copilot with commercial data protection, available for all students and staff members over the age of 18, and CurreChat, which is restricted for use by the staff and for teaching purposes. We recommend primarily using AI services supported by the University, such as Copilot with commercial data protection and CurreChat.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow teaching claim because the official teaching page directly states the general rule, teacher discretion, and maturity-test limit.
Approved because the official student guidance directly links prohibited or unreported LLM use to cheating and the official plagiarism page covers AI-generated work presented as one's own.
Approved as an AI tool treatment claim because the IT Helpdesk page names the two general-purpose services and recommends University-supported services.
Approved as a privacy claim because the official IT Helpdesk page directly distinguishes Copilot and CurreChat data categories.
Approved as a research claim because the official research-integrity page directly supports responsibility, verification, transparency, and no-coauthor wording.

artifacts.title: University of Helsinki AI Policy Source Review
artifacts.summary: Official University of Helsinki public guidance supports five staged claims covering AI use in teaching and writing support, academic-integrity treatment, University-supported AI services, data-protection limits for Copilot and CurreChat, and responsible GenAI use in research. The Flamma general AI principles page was inaccessible behind SSO, and a Faculty of Medicine thesis PDF was excluded from university-wide findings.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The staged report excludes login-gated Flamma content and does not infer university-wide rules from faculty-only PDF guidance.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
